name: mcp-tool-owns-its-hook-prompt
tags: mcp tool hook prompt injection additionalSystemPrompt ownership responsibility separation task:1ac0c420
body:
Each MCP tool builds its own __hook__ prompt — claude-hooks is a dumb pipe. Do not let the prompt-building logic live in the hook layer.

**Why:** The tool knows its own domain context (what iteration means, what confidence means, what the user should do next). The hook layer has no business interpreting that.

**How to apply:** When adding __hook__ to a new MCP tool, write the full additionalSystemPrompt string inside the tool function itself. The bridge node never touches the content.
